subject: Cloud Computing
title: SaaS: Software You Rent
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: SaaS: Software You Rent.

hook.text: Gmail, Slack, Zoom, Notion — you use them daily but never installed any of them on a server.

buildup.text: SaaS delivers complete applications over the internet. You pay a subscription and just use them.

discovery.text: The provider handles everything: servers, security, updates, backups. You just log in and work.

twist.text: SaaS is a $200 billion market. The average company uses over 100 different SaaS applications.

climax.text: SaaS turned software from a product you buy once into a subscription you pay forever.

punchline.text: SaaS: you never own it. You just keep paying for it.

quiz.question: What defines SaaS compared to other cloud models?
options: The provider delivers a complete application — the customer just uses it
Customers install the software on their own cloud servers
SaaS only works on mobile devices, not computers
